specific_change: Reduce the compute fleet from 4 × m5.large to 3 × m5.large by setting count = 3 in the aws_instance.compute resource. Keep the instance class unchanged (m5.large). Do not modify the database (db.r6g.large) or network (ALB with least_outstanding_requests) tiers, which are correctly sized.

conclusion.headline: Remove 1 of 4 m5.large instances to save ~$320/mo; other tiers healthy

telemetry_observations: Compute CPU p95 median is 39.1% with max 48.8%, well below saturation.
Compute CPU p50 median is only 23.1%, indicating mild under-utilization across 4 instances.
Memory p95 median is 39.4% (max 49.5%), leaving ample headroom.
Application p95 latency median is 133.8ms (max 171.3ms), far below the 500ms SLA target.
Database query p95 latency median is 55ms with cache hit ratio ≥0.92, healthy range.
Network error rate p95 is 0.009 (0.9%), well within acceptable bounds.
infrastructure_context: Compute is 4 × m5.large with no auto-scaling policy (fixed fleet).
Database is db.r6g.large with 1 replica and 200GB storage — appropriately sized for current load.
ALB uses least_outstanding_requests algorithm, suitable for the workload profile.

reasoning: Compute CPU utilization sits consistently below the healthy 30-50% band at p50 (23.1%) across all four instances, while p95 only reaches ~48%, indicating the fleet is mildly oversized. Removing one instance would redistribute load across three instances, lifting CPU p50 into the 30-40% range while keeping p95 comfortably below saturation. The application p95 latency at 133.8ms has over 300ms of headroom to the 500ms SLA, so a modest increase from the rebalance poses no SLA risk. Database and network tiers show healthy utilization and require no changes.

projected_state.notes: Estimates assume roughly linear redistribution of load across 3 instances (4/3 ≈ 1.33× per-instance load). Latency stays well within the 500ms SLA target.

cost_impact.notes: Compute tier drops from $1,280/mo (4 × m5.large) to $960/mo (3 × m5.large). Per-instance cost: $1,280 / 4 = $320/mo. Database ($720) and network ($400) costs unchanged.

risk_assessment.primary_risk: With only 3 instances, losing one instance leaves 2 serving all traffic, pushing CPU p95 potentially above 80% and increasing latency during the recovery window.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Set a CloudWatch alarm on CPU p95 > 70% with a 5-minute evaluation period. Pre-configure the launch template so a 4th instance can be launched within minutes. Consider adding a simple target-tracking auto-scaling policy (min=3, max=5) as a safety net.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert the Terraform count from 3 to 4 and apply. The original AMI and launch template are unchanged, so a new instance launches identically within minutes.
risk_assessment.notes: This is a tier-2 reporting dashboard (99.7% SLA), so brief capacity dips during instance replacement are tolerable.
